APPLE INC.
FORM 10-Q
For the Quarterly Period Ended April 1, 2023

ITEM 2. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS

The following discussion should be read in conjunction with the consolidated financial statements and accompanying notes included in Part I, Item 1 of this Form 10-Q.

OVERVIEW

The Company posted quarterly revenue of $94.8 billion for the second fiscal quarter of 2023, a decrease of 3% compared to the year-ago quarter. The Company generated operating cash flow of $28.6 billion during the second fiscal quarter of 2023.

Products net sales decreased 5% or $3.0 billion during the second fiscal quarter of 2023 compared to the year-ago quarter, primarily due to lower iPhone net sales. Services net sales increased 5% or $0.9 billion compared to the year-ago quarter, reflecting higher sales from advertising, the App Store and AppleCare.

NET SALES

Products
iPhone net sales were $51.3 billion in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, a decrease of $2.9 billion or 5% compared to the year-ago quarter, driven primarily by unfavorable foreign exchange rates and lower demand in certain markets.

Mac net sales were $7.2 billion in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, a decrease of $0.4 billion or 5% compared to the year-ago quarter, primarily driven by a difficult year-ago comparison due to the launch of the MacBook Pro models with the M1 Pro and M1 Max chips.

iPad net sales were $6.7 billion in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, an increase of $0.7 billion or 12% compared to the year-ago quarter, driven by favorable foreign exchange rates and new product launches.

Wearables, Home and Accessories net sales were $8.8 billion in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, a decrease of $0.4 billion or 4% compared to the year-ago quarter.

Services
Services net sales were $20.9 billion in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, an increase of $0.9 billion or 5% compared to the year-ago quarter. The growth was primarily driven by higher sales from advertising, the App Store, and AppleCare.

GROSS MARGIN

Gross margin was $42.6 billion for the second fiscal quarter of 2023, representing a gross margin percentage of 44.9%. This compares to gross margin of $43.9 billion and a gross margin percentage of 43.8% for the year-ago quarter. The increase in gross margin percentage was primarily driven by a different Products mix and higher Services net sales, partially offset by the weakness of foreign currencies relative to the U.S. dollar.

Products gross margin was $22.2 billion, representing a gross margin percentage of 36.7%. This compares to $22.7 billion and 35.7% in the year-ago quarter. The increase in Products gross margin percentage was primarily driven by a different mix of Products and cost savings, partially offset by the weakness of foreign currencies.

Services gross margin was $20.5 billion, representing a gross margin percentage of 98.2%. This compares to $21.2 billion and 98.9% in the year-ago quarter.

OPERATING EXPENSES

Research and development expense was $7.5 billion in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, an increase of $0.6 billion or 9% compared to the year-ago quarter.

Selling, general and administrative expense was $6.1 billion in the second fiscal quarter of 2023, a decrease of $0.1 billion or 2% compared to the year-ago quarter.

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

The following risk factors may be updated from the risk factors included in the Company's Annual Report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended September 24, 2022.

The Company faces significant macroeconomic uncertainty. The global economy continues to be affected by high inflation, rising interest rates, fluctuations in foreign exchange rates, and ongoing geopolitical tensions. These conditions have negatively impacted consumer spending and could adversely affect demand for our products and services.

The Company has significant exposure to foreign exchange risk. A substantial portion of the Company's revenues and expenses are denominated in currencies other than the U.S. dollar. Fluctuations in exchange rates between the U.S. dollar and other currencies could materially affect net sales, gross margin, and operating expenses.

Supply chain disruptions continue to pose operational risks. The Company relies on single-source or limited-source suppliers for some components. Disruptions in supply, including those caused by geopolitical tensions, natural disasters, or pandemics, could delay product availability and affect financial results.

The Company is subject to complex and changing laws and regulations. Changes in tax laws, data privacy regulations, competition law, and other regulatory requirements across the jurisdictions in which the Company operates may adversely affect its financial condition and results of operations.

LIQUIDITY AND CAPITAL RESOURCES

As of April 1, 2023, the Company had $55.2 billion in cash and cash equivalents and marketable securities, compared to $48.3 billion as of September 24, 2022. The Company's primary sources of liquidity are its cash flows from operating activities and its access to capital markets.

The Company generated $28.6 billion of cash from operations during the second fiscal quarter of 2023. Capital expenditures were $2.1 billion during the quarter.

During the second fiscal quarter of 2023, the Company returned $23.5 billion to shareholders through a combination of $3.7 billion in dividends and $19.8 billion in share repurchases.

The Company believes its existing cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities, together with its ability to generate cash from operations and access to capital markets, are sufficient to meet its anticipated liquidity needs.
